$comment: OB-5 — the authored Epoch-0 for a WEST_COAST/origins founding. The first stage of the story: a fully-written birth → the doctor's date → growing up on the boom-and-bust Pacific frontier (class/station emerging from a place where everyone is reinventing themselves), feeding into the shared gender → naming → calling → partner chain. Each choice sets a CAUSAL ANCHOR the butterfly/world engine reads. Place-gated to west_coast; higher weight than the generic beats so the Pacific line opens on its own prose. Culture: scots_irish. Cue: orange light, ocean fog, prospectors' pans.

events.title: Born in the Gold Light
events.scene: You arrive in a clapboard house thrown up in a hurry, in a town that did not exist ten years ago and may not exist in ten more. Fog rolls off the Pacific and burns away to a hard orange light by noon; somewhere down the gulch a man is still working a pan that long ago stopped paying. The doctor — a frontier physician who has set bones and pulled teeth and delivered the children of people running from somewhere else — wipes his spectacles and writes in a ledger he keeps for the whole valley. You hear him say the date aloud as he enters it.
events.researchNote: OB-5 birth opener for the West Coast. Sets `emerged` so the Epoch-0 chain begins; narrates the seed-drawn date via {birth_date}. Boom-town Pacific frontier, 1885 — a place of reinvention, where the gold rush has crested and the next thing is being invented.

choices.text: You listen to the doctor enter the day into his valley-wide book.
choices.outcome: “{birth_date},” the doctor reads back, and snaps the ledger shut. Out here a birth is just one more line in a country still writing itself — and a country still writing itself has room for a name that isn't on any page yet.

events.title: A Name for a New Country
events.scene: The {family_name} name came west in a wagon or a steerage berth — but the given name is decided here, in a place where a person can be whoever they say they are. Some keep the old country alive in the christening; some bury it on purpose. The choice is made over a kitchen table that still smells of fresh-cut pine, and you become {full_name}.
events.researchNote: OB-5: given-naming on the frontier, where reinvention is the local religion. The given name was dealt from the scots_irish pool at founding; this beat is the bestowal that sets `named` and frames how the name was chosen — an early personality anchor distinct from Ireland's saint-and-parish framing.

choices.text: Named to keep the old country alive — a thread back to who the line was.
choices.outcome: An old-world name in a brand-new town. The past comes west too, whether the frontier wants it or not.

choices.text: Named for luck and bigness — a name to match the country's ambitions.
choices.outcome: A name picked the way a claim is staked — for what it might one day be worth. The West rewards nerve, and the naming starts early.

choices.text: A plain American name — no past to explain, no airs to keep up.
choices.outcome: No saint, no fortune — just a clean name for a fresh start. Out here that is its own kind of freedom.

events.title: What the Boom Teaches
events.scene: You are old enough now to read the town. Money comes and goes like the fog — a strike, a railroad spur, a bank that opens loud and closes quiet. The men who do well are not always the strongest or the most honest; they are the ones who saw it coming and got there first. You watch a neighbor go from a shack to a hill house in three years, and another go the opposite way just as fast, and you begin to understand that out here the world is not fixed — it can be MOVED, if you find the lever.
events.researchNote: OB-5: class/station emerges early as an organic response to observing the form + function of frontier life (user). Boom-and-bust Pacific economy — the lesson is mobility and timing, not the fixed tenant-and-landlord order of Ireland. Sets an early-pressure anchor the calling reads.

choices.text: You catch the boom-town fever — you mean to be the one who gets there first.
choices.outcome: Some children learn caution from the busts. You learn appetite from the booms. The wanting will not leave you now.

choices.text: You study the men who saw it coming — the lever, not the luck.
choices.outcome: You see that the fortune isn't in the gold — it's in knowing where the gold will be, and owning the road to it. You mean to be early, every time.

choices.text: You trust honest work over the gamble, like the steadiest folk in town.
choices.outcome: There is a dignity in building something the busts can't wash away. Whether steadiness alone can found a line out here is a question for later years.

events.title: The Schoolhouse and the Drifter
events.scene: There is a one-room schoolhouse and a teacher, Miss Reyes, who came west for reasons she does not discuss and stayed for reasons she cannot name. She has seen clever children leave for the city and dull ones strike it rich, and she has stopped pretending she knows which is which. "You've got something," she tells you, weighing you up like a claim. "The question out here is never whether you've got it. It's whether you'll spend it on the right thing before the town spends it for you."
events.researchNote: OB-5: the calling EMERGES (user) — not one static pick. Schooling + a worldly, disillusioned-but-perceptive frontier teacher is the first pressure that bends the child toward a bent. Sets `schooled`, an anchor the crystallization reads. Distinct in tone from Ireland's embittered Mr. Lynch — Miss Reyes is a fellow reinventor.

choices.text: You read everything she can find you — hungry for the wider world coming.
choices.outcome: Miss Reyes starts ordering books from the city just to keep ahead of you. A door has opened that the frontier cannot close — because the frontier itself is a door.

choices.text: You learn the people — every drifter and dealmaker who passes through.
choices.outcome: You discover you can read a stranger in a minute and turn a room your way in five. In a country of reinventors, that is the sharpest gift there is.

choices.text: You leave the slate for paying work — the town runs on hands, not lessons.
choices.outcome: Miss Reyes lets you go without a fight; out here she has seen it pay off as often as not. The work does not care what you might have been — only what you can haul.

events.title: What You Are For
events.scene: You are a young {given_name} {surname} now, grown into the body and the wide-open country that made you. The frontier's loose pulls have hardened into a direction. Out here a person is whatever they decide to be — but they have to DECIDE, or the country decides for them, and it is not gentle with the undecided. Standing at the edge of your own life, with the Pacific at your back and a continent of nerve in front of you, you feel which way you will go.
events.researchNote: OB-5: the calling CRYSTALLIZES into the line's archetype (user: calling IS the archetype, and it EMERGES). All six archetypes offered; setsArchetype commits the power base. The prior schooling/station anchors color which feel natural. Replaces the generic ev_birth_calling for the West Coast.

choices.text: Money. You will own the land, the water, and the road everyone else has to pay to use.
choices.outcome: The Magnate's road: the West does not ask where your money came from, only how much there is. The line will own the country's bones — the rails, the rivers, the rights-of-way.

choices.text: Power. You will run the new state before the old families even know it has one.
choices.outcome: The Statesman's road: in a country this young, the offices are still up for grabs and the rules still soft. The line will write them — and be written into them.

choices.text: Faith. You will give a rootless, reinvented people something to believe in.
choices.outcome: The Prophet's road: nowhere hungers for meaning like a place where no one is from here. The line will gather the lost and the seeking — and the standing that comes with their faith.

choices.text: Invention. You will build the machines this empty country is waiting for.
choices.outcome: The Visionary's road: the West is a blank page for anyone with a wrench and a notion. The line will make what does not yet exist, and the future will be built on the coast.

choices.text: The crowd. You have the kind of face and nerve the new picture-shows are built around.
choices.outcome: The Star's road: the stage, the screen, the dream-machine the coast will become famous for. The line will be looked at by millions — and looked at is its own kind of power, especially here.

choices.text: The body. You are the fastest, hardest thing the valley ever raised, and crowds will pay to watch.
choices.outcome: The Champion's road: the ring, the track, the field a frontier crowd turns out for in its thousands. The line will win its name with raw ability — earned in front of everyone, owned by no one.
